Below is a consolidated extraction of the key terms for KKR & Co. Inc.’s **6.25% Series D Mandatory Convertible Preferred Stock** from the **final prospectus supplement filed under Rule 424(b)(5)** and the **Certificate of Designations (Exhibit 3.1)**, with relevant quotes and source sections.

## Summary of key terms

| Term | Extracted term / value | Source |
|---|---:|---|
| Issuer | KKR & Co. Inc. | Prospectus supplement cover; Certificate recitals |
| Security | “6.25% Series D Mandatory Convertible Preferred Stock,” par value $0.01 per share | Prospectus supplement cover; Certificate §1 |
| Offering size | **45,000,000 shares** | Prospectus supplement cover / “The Offering”; FWP |
| Authorized Series D shares in certificate | **51,750,000 shares** | Certificate §1 |
| Public offering price | **$50.00 per share** | Prospectus supplement cover / “The Offering”; FWP |
| Total gross offering | **$2,250,000,000** | Prospectus supplement cover |
| Underwriting discount | **$1.125 per share; $50,625,000 total** | Prospectus supplement cover / Underwriting |
| Proceeds before expenses | **$48.875 per share; $2,199,375,000 total** | Prospectus supplement cover |
| Estimated net proceeds | **Approx. $2.20 billion**, or **approx. $2.53 billion** if option exercised in full | “Use of Proceeds”; FWP |
| Closing / settlement / initial issue date | **March 7, 2025** | Prospectus supplement cover / “Settlement Date”; Certificate definition of “Initial Issue Date” |
| Liquidation preference | **$50.00 per share** | “The Offering”; Certificate definitions / §5 |
| Dividend rate | **6.25% per annum** on $50 liquidation preference = **$3.125 per annum per share** | “Dividends”; Certificate §4(a) |
| Dividend payment dates | **March 1, June 1, September 1, December 1**, commencing **June 1, 2025**, through and including **March 1, 2028** | “Dividend Payment Dates”; Certificate definition / §4(a) |
| Dividend record dates | **February 15, May 15, August 15, November 15** | “Dividends”; Certificate definition of Regular Record Date |
| First dividend | Approx. **$0.7292 per share** | “Dividends”; Certificate §4(a) |
| Subsequent quarterly dividend | Expected **$0.78125 per share** | “Dividends”; Certificate §4(a) |
| Mandatory conversion date | Expected **March 1, 2028**; legally, “second Business Day immediately following the last Trading Day of the Settlement Period” | “Mandatory Conversion Date”; Certificate definition |
| Settlement Period | 20 consecutive Trading Day period beginning on and including the 21st Scheduled Trading Day immediately preceding March 1, 2028 | “Conversion Rate”; Certificate definitions |
| Minimum conversion rate | **0.3312 shares of common stock per preferred share** | “Conversion Rate”; Certificate §8(b)(i) |
| Maximum conversion rate | **0.4140 shares of common stock per preferred share** | “Conversion Rate”; Certificate §8(b)(iii) |
| Initial Price | Approx. **$120.7729** | “Conversion Rate”; Certificate definition |
| Threshold Appreciation Price | Approx. **$150.9662**, approx. 25% above Initial Price | “Conversion Rate”; Certificate definition |
| Floor Price | **$42.2705**, approx. 35% of Initial Price | “Dividends”; Certificate §4(e) |
| Early conversion | At holder option any time before March 1, 2028, other than during Fundamental Change Conversion Period, at Minimum Conversion Rate | “Early Conversion at the Option of the Holder”; Certificate §9 |
| Fundamental change conversion | If Fundamental Change occurs on or before March 1, 2028, holders may convert during Fundamental Change Conversion Period at Fundamental Change Conversion Rate and receive make-whole / accumulated dividend amounts | “Conversion at the Option of the Holder upon Fundamental Change”; Certificate §10 |
| Fundamental Change make-whole discount rate | **4.77% per annum** | “Fundamental Change Dividend Make-Whole Amount”; Certificate §10(a)(ii) |
| Voting rights | Generally none, except required by law / certificate; special director voting after six unpaid dividend periods; two-thirds consent for certain adverse actions | “Voting Power”; Certificate §7 |
| Redemption | Not redeemable; no sinking fund | “Redemption”; Certificate §6 |
| Use of proceeds | Acquire additional equity interests in core private equity portfolio companies in Strategic Holdings segment and general corporate purposes | Cover; “Use of Proceeds” |
| Underwriters’ option | Up to **6,750,000 additional shares**, exercisable within 30 days, solely to cover over-allotments | Cover; “The Offering”; Underwriting |
| Listing / ticker | Intends to list preferred on NYSE under **“KKR PR D”**; common stock listed NYSE under **“KKR”** | Cover; “Listing”; Certificate §22 |
| CUSIP / ISIN | **48251W 500 / US48251W5004** | FWP; Certificate Exhibit A |
| Transfer agent / registrar | Equiniti Trust Company, LLC | “Transfer Agent and Registrar”; Certificate §16 |

---

## Offering size, price, closing and proceeds

**Offering size.** The final prospectus supplement states on the cover: **“45,000,000 Shares 6.25% Series D Mandatory Convertible Preferred Stock.”** In “The Offering,” KKR repeats: **“45,000,000 shares of our 6.25% Series D Mandatory Convertible Preferred Stock, par value $0.01 per share.”**

The Certificate of Designations authorizes the full base plus option amount: **“a series of Preferred Stock consisting of 51,750,000 shares … designated as the ‘6.25% Series D Mandatory Convertible Preferred Stock’”**.  
**Source:** Prospectus supplement cover; “The Offering”; Certificate §1.

**Public offering price and gross amount.** The cover table provides:

> “Public offering price — **$50.000** per share; **$2,250,000,000** total.”  
> “Underwriting discount — **$1.125** per share; **$50,625,000** total.”  
> “Proceeds, before expenses, to us — **$48.875** per share; **$2,199,375,000** total.”

**Closing / settlement date.** The prospectus supplement says:

> “The underwriters expect to deliver the Mandatory Convertible Preferred Stock to purchasers on or about **March 7, 2025**, which will be the second business day after the initial trade date … referred to as ‘T+2.’”

The Certificate defines:

> “Initial Issue Date” means **“March 7, 2025, the first original issue date of shares of the Mandatory Convertible Preferred Stock.”**

**Source:** Prospectus supplement cover; “The Offering—Settlement Date”; Certificate definitions.

**Net proceeds.** Under “Use of Proceeds,” KKR states:

> “We estimate that the net proceeds to us from this offering, after deducting underwriting discounts and estimated offering expenses payable by us, will be approximately **$2.20 billion** (or approximately **$2.53 billion** if the underwriters exercise their over-allotment option … in full).”

**Source:** “Use of Proceeds,” p. S-32; FWP.

---

## Dividends

**Rate and preference base.** The prospectus supplement states:

> “Dividends … will be payable on a cumulative basis when, as and if declared … at an annual rate of **6.25%** on the liquidation preference of **$50.00 per share**.”

The Certificate states:

> “cumulative dividends at the rate per annum of **6.25% of the Liquidation Preference** … equivalent to **$3.125 per annum per share**.”

**Payment dates.** Dividends are payable:

> “on **March 1, June 1, September 1 and December 1** of each year, commencing on **June 1, 2025**, and ending on, and including, **March 1, 2028**.”

**Record dates.** The Certificate defines Regular Record Dates as:

> “the **February 15, May 15, August 15 and November 15**, as the case may be, immediately preceding the relevant Dividend Payment Date.”

**First and subsequent dividends.** The prospectus supplement states:

> “The expected dividend payable on the first dividend payment date is approximately **$0.7292 per share** … Each subsequent dividend is expected to be **$0.78125 per share**.”

**Accumulation.** The Certificate provides dividends:

> “shall accumulate from the most recent date as to which dividends shall have been paid or, if no dividends have been paid, from the Initial Issue Date, whether or not in any Dividend Period … there have been funds legally available or shares of Common Stock legally permitted to be issued.”

Accumulated dividends:

> “shall not bear interest, nor shall additional dividends be payable thereon, if they are paid subsequent to the applicable Dividend Payment Date.”

**Payment form.** KKR may pay declared dividends:

> “in cash, by delivery of shares of Common Stock or through any combination of cash and shares of Common Stock,”

as determined by KKR in its sole discretion, subject to limitations. If paid in common stock, the shares are valued at:

> “**97% of the Average VWAP** per share of Common Stock over the five consecutive Trading Day period beginning on, and including, the sixth Scheduled Trading Day immediately preceding the applicable Dividend Payment Date.”

The number of common shares delivered for a dividend is capped using the **Floor Price**:

> “$42.2705, subject to adjustment … the ‘Floor Price.’”

**Source:** Prospectus supplement cover; “The Offering—Dividends”; “Description of Mandatory Convertible Preferred Stock—Dividends”; Certificate §4.

---

## Liquidation preference and ranking

**Liquidation preference.** The Certificate defines:

> “Liquidation Preference” means, as to Mandatory Convertible Preferred Stock, **$50.00 per share**.

On liquidation, holders receive:

> “the Liquidation Preference of **$50.00 per share** … plus … accumulated and unpaid dividends … to, but excluding, the date fixed for liquidation, winding-up or dissolution.”

**Ranking.** The Mandatory Convertible Preferred Stock ranks:

> “senior to … Common Stock … Existing Series I Preferred Stock … and each other class or series of Junior Stock,”  
> “on parity with … Parity Stock,”  
> “junior to … Senior Stock,” and  
> “junior to the Corporation’s existing and future indebtedness and other liabilities.”

It is also:

> “structurally subordinated to any existing and future indebtedness and other liabilities of each of its Subsidiaries.”

**Source:** “Description of Mandatory Convertible Preferred Stock—Ranking”; Certificate §2 and §5.

---

## Mandatory conversion mechanics

Unless earlier converted, each share automatically converts on the Mandatory Conversion Date. The Certificate states:

> “Each outstanding share … shall automatically convert … on the Mandatory Conversion Date … into a number of shares of Common Stock equal to the Mandatory Conversion Rate.”

**Mandatory Conversion Date.** Defined as:

> “the second Business Day immediately following the last Trading Day of the Settlement Period. The Mandatory Conversion Date is expected to be **March 1, 2028**.”

**Settlement Period.**

> “the **20 consecutive Trading Day** period beginning on, and including, the **21st Scheduled Trading Day immediately preceding March 1, 2028**.”

**Conversion rates and thresholds.**

| Applicable Market Value of KKR common stock | Conversion rate |
|---|---:|
| Greater than Threshold Appreciation Price | **0.3312** shares common per preferred share |
| Equal to or less than Threshold Appreciation Price but equal to or greater than Initial Price | **$50.00 / Applicable Market Value**, rounded to nearest 1/10,000th |
| Less than Initial Price | **0.4140** shares common per preferred share |

**Initial Price.** The Certificate defines:

> “Initial Price” means **$50.00, divided by the Maximum Conversion Rate**, which quotient is initially equal to approximately **$120.7729**.

**Threshold Appreciation Price.**

> “Threshold Appreciation Price” means **$50.00, divided by the Minimum Conversion Rate**, which quotient is initially equal to approximately **$150.9662**.

The prospectus supplement notes that the Threshold Appreciation Price represents:

> “approximately **25% appreciation** over the Initial Price.”

**Source:** “Description of Mandatory Convertible Preferred Stock—Mandatory Conversion”; Certificate §8 and definitions.

---

## Early conversion at holder option

The Certificate provides:

> “Other than during a Fundamental Change Conversion Period … Holders shall have the option to convert their Mandatory Convertible Preferred Stock, in whole or in part … at any time prior to **March 1, 2028** … into shares of Common Stock at the **Minimum Conversion Rate**.”

The prospectus supplement similarly states holders may convert:

> “at any time prior to March 1, 2028 … at the Minimum Conversion Rate of **0.3312 shares** of our common stock per share.”

If KKR has undeclared accumulated and unpaid dividends for full dividend periods ending on or before the immediately prior Dividend Payment Date, the conversion rate is adjusted by an additional amount:

> “Early Conversion Additional Amount” divided by “the greater of (x) the Floor Price and (y) the Average VWAP … over the 20 consecutive Trading Day period … immediately preceding the Early Conversion Date.”

But:

> “To the extent that the Early Conversion Additional Amount exceeds the product of such number of additional shares and the Early Conversion Average Price, we will not have any obligation to pay the shortfall in cash or deliver shares …”

**Source:** “Description of Mandatory Convertible Preferred Stock—Early Conversion at the Option of the Holder”; Certificate §9.

---

## Fundamental change conversion

If a Fundamental Change occurs on or before March 1, 2028, holders have a special conversion right. The Certificate states holders have the right during the Fundamental Change Conversion Period to:

> “convert their shares … into a number of shares of Common Stock … equal to the Fundamental Change Conversion Rate,”  
> receive the **“Fundamental Change Dividend Make-Whole Amount,”** and  
> receive the **“Accumulated Dividend Amount”** if any.

**Fundamental Change Conversion Period.** The Certificate defines it as:

> “the period beginning on, and including, the Fundamental Change Effective Date and ending at the Close of Business on the date that is **20 calendar days** after the Fundamental Change Effective Date … but in no event later than **March 1, 2028**.”

**Make-whole amount.** The prospectus supplement states holders converting during the period receive:

> “a ‘Fundamental Change Dividend Make-Whole Amount’ equal to the present value, calculated using a discount rate of **4.77% per annum**, of all dividend payments …”

The Certificate uses the same **4.77% per annum** discount rate.

**Fundamental Change definition.** A Fundamental Change includes, subject to exceptions:

1. Certain recapitalizations, reclassifications, mergers, consolidations, binding share exchanges, or sales / transfers of all or substantially all assets;
2. A person or group becoming beneficial owner of more than **50%** of total voting power; or
3. KKR common stock ceasing to be listed or quoted on NYSE, Nasdaq Global Select Market, Nasdaq Global Market, or another U.S. national securities exchange.

The Certificate excludes:

> “any event or action contemplated by, or taken in furtherance of consummating the transactions contemplated by and in compliance with, the Reorganization Agreement …”

**Fundamental Change Conversion Rate table.** The Certificate and FWP set the following rates per preferred share:

| Fundamental Change Effective Date | $20 | $40 | $60 | $80 | $100 | $120.77 | $140 | $150.97 | $180 | $200 | $220 | $240 | $260 | $280 | $300 |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| Mar. 7, 2025 | 0.3681 | 0.3859 | 0.3823 | 0.3719 | 0.3608 | 0.3512 | 0.3444 | 0.3413 | 0.3355 | 0.3329 | 0.3311 | 0.3298 | 0.3290 | 0.3284 | 0.3280 |
| Mar. 1, 2026 | 0.3827 | 0.3963 | 0.3947 | 0.3845 | 0.3714 | 0.3587 | 0.3495 | 0.3453 | 0.3375 | 0.3343 | 0.3321 | 0.3307 | 0.3298 | 0.3293 | 0.3289 |
| Mar. 1, 2027 | 0.3980 | 0.4055 | 0.4067 | 0.4008 | 0.3870 | 0.3695 | 0.3553 | 0.3490 | 0.3380 | 0.3340 | 0.3319 | 0.3307 | 0.3302 | 0.3299 | 0.3298 |
| Mar. 1, 2028 | 0.4140 | 0.4140 | 0.4140 | 0.4140 | 0.4140 | 0.4140 | 0.3571 | 0.3312 | 0.3312 | 0.3312 | 0.3312 | 0.3312 | 0.3312 | 0.3312 | 0.3312 |

If the exact stock price or effective date is not in the table, the rate is determined by straight-line interpolation. If the Fundamental Change Stock Price is above **$300.00**, the rate is the Minimum Conversion Rate; if below **$20.00**, the rate is the Maximum Conversion Rate, in each case subject to adjustment.

**Source:** “Conversion at the Option of the Holder upon Fundamental Change; Fundamental Change Dividend Make-Whole Amount”; Certificate §10 and definitions; FWP.

---

## Voting rights

The prospectus supplement states:

> “Except as specifically required by Delaware law or our certificate of incorporation, the holders of the Mandatory Convertible Preferred Stock will have no voting rights or powers.”

The Certificate similarly states:

> “Holders shall not have any voting rights or powers other than those set forth in this Section 7, except as specifically required by Delaware law or by the Charter.”

**Director voting right after dividend nonpayment.** If dividends are unpaid for six or more dividend periods:

> “Whenever dividends … have not been declared and paid for the equivalent of **six or more Dividend Periods**, whether or not for consecutive Dividend Periods … the authorized number of directors … shall … automatically be increased by two,”

and holders, voting with other Voting Preferred Stock, may elect:

> “a total of **two additional members** of the Board of Directors.”

This right terminates when:

> “all accumulated and unpaid dividends … have been paid in full, or declared and a sum or number of shares of the Common Stock sufficient for such payment shall have been set aside.”

**Protective voting rights.** So long as any Series D shares are outstanding, KKR cannot, without approval of holders of at least **two-thirds in voting power** of the outstanding Series D shares, and in certain cases voting with other Voting Preferred Stock:

1. Amend the charter to authorize, create or increase Senior Stock;
2. Amend, alter or repeal the charter or Certificate of Designations so as to materially and adversely affect the special rights, preferences or voting powers of Series D; or
3. Consummate certain binding share exchanges, reclassifications, mergers or consolidations unless the Series D remains outstanding or is converted into preference securities with rights not materially less favorable.

**Source:** “Voting Power”; Certificate §7.

---

## Redemption / sinking fund

The preferred stock is **not redeemable**. The Certificate states:

> “The Mandatory Convertible Preferred Stock shall not be subject to any redemption, sinking fund or other similar provisions.”

KKR may, however:

> “purchase or exchange the Mandatory Convertible Preferred Stock from time to time in the open market, by tender or exchange offer or otherwise, without the consent of, or notice to, Holders.”

**Source:** “Redemption”; Certificate §6.

---

## Use of proceeds / purpose

The cover states:

> “We intend to use the net proceeds from this offering for the acquisition of additional equity interests in core private equity portfolio companies reported in our Strategic Holdings segment and for other general corporate purposes.”

The “Use of Proceeds” section repeats:

> “We intend to use the net proceeds from this offering for the acquisition of additional equity interests in core private equity portfolio companies reported in our Strategic Holdings segment and for other general corporate purposes.”

The summary also notes KKR announced on February 4, 2025 that it was increasing ownership interests in certain core private equity businesses within Strategic Holdings by approximately **$1.1 billion**, anticipated to complete in the first half of 2025.

**Source:** Prospectus supplement cover; “Use of Proceeds,” p. S-32; “Summary—Recent Developments.”

---

## Underwriters’ over-allotment option

KKR granted the underwriters:

> “the option, exercisable within **30 days** from the date of this prospectus supplement, to purchase up to an additional **6,750,000 shares** … at the public offering price, less the underwriting discount, solely to cover over-allotments, if any.”

**Source:** Prospectus supplement cover; “The Offering—Underwriters’ Option”; “Underwriting.”

---

## Listing, ticker, CUSIP and transfer agent

**Preferred listing.** KKR states:

> “We intend to apply to list the Mandatory Convertible Preferred Stock on the New York Stock Exchange … under the symbol **‘KKR PR D.’** If the application is approved, we expect trading … to begin within **30 days** after the Mandatory Convertible Preferred Stock is first issued.”

The Certificate provides that if listed, KKR will use commercially reasonable efforts to keep it listed on NYSE or the principal exchange on which common stock is listed.  
**Source:** Prospectus supplement cover; “Listing”; Certificate §22.

**Common stock listing.**

> “Our common stock is listed on the NYSE under the ticker symbol **‘KKR.’**”

**CUSIP / ISIN.** The FWP and Certificate Exhibit A list:

> CUSIP **48251W 500**; ISIN **US48251W5004**.

**Transfer agent / registrar.**

> “Equiniti Trust Company, LLC is the transfer agent, registrar and conversion and dividend disbursing agent for the Mandatory Convertible Preferred Stock.”

**Source:** “Transfer Agent and Registrar”; Certificate §16 and Exhibit A.

steps.args:
{'final_result': 'KKR & Co. Inc. (NYSE: KKR) priced an upsized March 2025 offering of **45,000,000 shares** of **6.25% Series D Mandatory Convertible Preferred Stock** at **$50.00 per share**, for **$2.25 billion gross proceeds**. It had initially announced a $1.50 billion offering, but the priced transaction was upsized. The underwriters also received a 30-day option to buy up to **6,750,000 additional shares** — **$337.50 million** liquidation preference — solely to cover over-allotments.

### Key terms

| Term | Summary |
|---|---|
| **Issuer / security** | KKR & Co. Inc.; **6.25% Series D Mandatory Convertible Preferred Stock**, par value $0.01 per share. |
| **Offering size** | **45.00 million shares**; **$2.25 billion** aggregate public offering price / liquidation preference. Plus underwriters’ 30-day option for **6.75 million additional shares**. |
| **Closing / initial issue date** | Expected / initial issue date: **March 7, 2025**. |
| **Price** | **$50.00 per preferred share**. Underwriting discount: **$1.125 per share**; proceeds before expenses: **$48.875 per share**. Estimated net proceeds: about **$2.20 billion**, or about **$2.53 billion** if the over-allotment option were exercised in full. |
| **Liquidation preference** | **$50.00 per share**, plus accumulated and unpaid dividends upon liquidation, winding up or dissolution. |
| **Dividend rights** | Cumulative dividends at **6.25% per year** on the $50.00 liquidation preference, equal to **$3.125 per year per share**. Dividends payable when, as and if declared on **March 1, June 1, September 1 and December 1**, beginning **June 1, 2025** and ending on and including **March 1, 2028**. First dividend expected about **$0.7292 per share**; later quarterly dividends expected **$0.78125 per share**. KKR may pay declared dividends in cash, common stock, or a combination, subject to the certificate’s limits. |
| **Mandatory conversion** | Unless earlier converted, each preferred share automatically converts on the mandatory conversion date, expected **March 1, 2028**. Conversion is based on the average KKR common-stock price during a 20-trading-day settlement period. |
| **Conversion rates** | Initial **minimum conversion rate: 0.3312** KKR common shares per preferred share. Initial **maximum conversion rate: 0.4140** KKR common shares per preferred share. These imply an **initial price of about $120.7729** and a **threshold appreciation price of about $150.9662**, or about **25.00%** above the initial price. If the applicable market value is above the threshold price, holders receive 0.3312 shares; if it is between the initial price and threshold price, holders receive $50 divided by the applicable market value; if below the initial price, holders receive 0.4140 shares. |
| **Early conversion** | Holders may convert before March 1, 2028, other than during a fundamental-change conversion period, at the **minimum conversion rate of 0.3312** shares per preferred share, with adjustments for certain accumulated unpaid dividends. |
| **Fundamental change conversion** | If a fundamental change occurs on or before March 1, 2028, holders may convert during the applicable conversion period at a fundamental-change conversion rate and receive a dividend make-whole amount. The dividend make-whole is discounted at **4.77% per year**. |
| **Voting rights** | Generally **no voting rights**, except as required by Delaware law or KKR’s charter / certificate. If dividends are unpaid for the equivalent of **six or more dividend periods**, holders, voting with other applicable preferred holders, may elect **two additional directors**. KKR also needs approval of holders of at least **two-thirds** of the outstanding Series D preferred, voting as specified, for certain adverse charter amendments, creation / increase of senior stock, or certain merger / exchange transactions unless protections are preserved. |
| **Redemption** | **Not redeemable**; no sinking fund. KKR may repurchase or exchange the preferred stock in the market or otherwise. |
| **Use of proceeds / purpose** | KKR intended to use net proceeds to **acquire additional equity interests in core private equity portfolio companies reported in its Strategic Holdings segment** and for **other general corporate purposes**. |
| **Listing** | KKR intended to list the Series D preferred on the NYSE under **“KKR PR D”**; KKR common stock trades under **“KKR.”** |

### Bottom line

As of April 7, 2025, the March 2025 Series D transaction was an upsized **$2.25 billion mandatory convertible preferred financing** with a **6.25% cumulative dividend**, **$50.00 liquidation preference**, and mandatory conversion into KKR common stock expected on **March 1, 2028** at a rate ranging initially from **0.3312 to 0.4140 common shares** per preferred share. Economically, investors receive preferred dividends for roughly three years and then convert into KKR equity, while KKR raised roughly **$2.20 billion net** primarily to increase ownership in Strategic Holdings portfolio companies.

      "url": "https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1404912/000114036125007426/ny20042797x2_424b5.htm",
      "name": "KKR & Co. Inc. Rule 424(b)(5) Final Prospectus Supplement filed March 6, 2025"
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1404912/000114036125007608/ny20042797x5_ex3-1.htm",
      "name": "Certificate of Designations for 6.25% Series D Mandatory Convertible Preferred Stock, Exhibit 3.1 to KKR Form 8-K filed March 7, 2025"
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1404912/000114036125007061/ny20042797x3_fwp.htm",
      "name": "KKR Free Writing Prospectus / Pricing Term Sheet filed March 5, 2025"
    }
  ]
}'}
